response.text:
# REFLECTION

r2c1 is in critical survival mode with SoC at 11.5% (0.23/2 kWh) and islanded operation, facing depletion in ~14 minutes at current 1.00 kW load. The recent message history shows a consistent rejection pattern from all geographic neighbors (r1c1, r2c0, r3c1), each citing their own conservation thresholds and cluster stress. Notably, r3c1 did offer a minimal 0.003125 kWh transfer, suggesting some peers recognize the urgency but are severely constrained. With no trust circles established and no visible generation, r2c1 must shift from a balanced sharing posture to aggressive inbound-seeking behavior while simultaneously entering self-preservation mode to maximize remaining autonomy. The fallback geographic round-robin policy has proven ineffective; a survival-oriented strategy requesting urgent assistance from any available peer, while accepting even minimal transfers, is now the only viable path to avoid complete depletion.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.85
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "CRITICAL SURVIVAL MODE: SoC 11.5%, islanded, ~14 min to depletion. All neighbors rejecting standard requests. Suspending all outbound transfers; accepting any inbound offer. Requesting urgent assistance from geographic circle with survival justification. No generation visible; load shedding may be required if no peer responds within 2 ticks."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
